title: Craspedia richea
intro: Craspedia richea, also known as Billy buttons, is a small, annual flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its clusters of small, white or yellow flowers resembling a button. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Craspedia richea include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Craspedia richea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.

The species name 'richea' is derived from the Latinized form of the name 'Richie', which is often associated with the New Zealand flora. As a member of the Apiaceae family, Craspedia richea is closely related to other plants such as celery and angelica.
